shared_properties: The obstacle uses the same explicit wood palette family (#5C3A1E, #8B5E3C, #D4A96A) and flat shading logic defined for branch hazards in this package.
The branch preserves a low collision-friendly profile for fair lane judgement.
Twig detail remains secondary to the readable trunk mass.
differences: This variant adds a heavy side knot that helps players read lateral motion instantly.
The sprite is authored for the late-stage moving obstacle rule rather than the static lane-gate rule.
The silhouette stays compact at 280x16px so code-driven motion supplies the extra difficulty rather than added visual bulk.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the core loop, timing windows, obstacle pacing, UI layout, and Bevy target constraints.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the sepia autumn atmosphere, tree silhouettes, and warm color layering.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteBundle obstacle entity with a fixed art asset and code-driven sinusoidal transform motion.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load obstacles/obstacles_branch_gate_swaying_static_280x16.png during stage setup and pair it with motion parameters for ±120px horizontal travel over a 0.8 second cycle.
runtime_notes: Drive movement through code-side transform updates while the painted branch silhouette remains static.
Keep the collision box synchronized with the sprite origin through the full sway range and snap the transform to whole pixels.
Restrict spawn use to 1700px depth onward so the motion pattern aligns with the BPM escalation phase.

context.usage: Late-stage branch gate that slides left and right while keeping a stable readable silhouette.

shape.silhouette: Broad branch with a weighted knot on one side to make horizontal sway direction easy to read.

acceptance_criteria: PNG canvas is exactly 280x16px with transparent padding removed from the outer bounds.
One side includes a visibly heavier knot so lateral motion direction reads within one frame.
The branch silhouette stays clear when composited over the red danger vignette and the near tree layer.
